headline: Constellation Energy Q1 beats: EPS $2.74 vs $2.59; reaffirms FY26 guide $11-$12, targets 20%+ growth through 2029
summary: CEG posted Q1 adj EPS of $2.74 on revenue of $11.12B (vs ~$9B consensus), boosted by the Calpine acquisition that closed in January. Reaffirmed FY26 adj operating earnings guidance of $11.00-$12.00. Outlined a strategic framework targeting base EPS growth of more than 20% from 2026 through 2029, with long-term three-year rolling growth of 10%+. Shares rose ~4% pre-market.

headline: SK Hynix surges 15% to all-time high; market cap tops $900B as UBS lifts target to KRW 1.7M
summary: 005930.KS rival SK Hynix (000660.KS) hit a record intraday on a UBS upgrade. UBS raised PT from KRW 1.55M to KRW 1.7M and lifted 2026/27 EPS estimates by 22%/29%, citing a memory super-cycle 'unseen in nearly 30 years' with HBM capacity sold out. The move pulls the whole memory complex higher (MU, 005930.KS).

headline: Samsung passes Nvidia and AMD final HBM4 qualification; full-scale supply begins June, 4nm HBM4 logic die prices up 40-50%
summary: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) has cleared the last quality gate for HBM4 with both NVIDIA and AMD and is set to begin full-scale shipments in June. The company raised foundry pricing for the 4nm HBM4 logic die by 40-50%, signaling pricing power in a tight market and validating Samsung's catch-up vs SK Hynix. Samsung is also targeting ~50% HBM capacity expansion in 2026.

headline: Intel CEO Tan teases 'exciting new products' with Nvidia; Apple foundry deal lifts INTC pre-market
summary: Intel (INTC) shares rose Monday after CEO Lip-Bu Tan publicly teased new datacenter and PC products with Nvidia at a Carnegie Mellon ceremony, building on the $5B Nvidia equity investment and joint x86-RTX SOC roadmap announced last year. Separately, Apple has signed a preliminary foundry agreement with Intel for some chip manufacturing, the strongest signal yet that Intel Foundry is scaling. Both items support the INTC AI/foundry thesis.

headline: Nvidia + IREN: $2.1B equity right plus $3.4B 5-year cloud contract, up to 5 GW DSX-aligned datacenter buildout
summary: NVDA's wave of strategic AI-infra investments crossed $40B in commitments YTD with the IREN deal announced last week: a five-year right to purchase 30M IREN shares at $70 (up to $2.1B) and a separate $3.4B five-year cloud services contract anchored by air-cooled Blackwell at IREN's Childress, TX campus. The pair are targeting up to 5 GW of DSX-aligned AI infrastructure, with IREN's 2 GW Sweetwater, TX campus as the primary build site. Reinforces NVDA's role as both supplier and investor in the neocloud layer.

headline: CoreWeave Q1: $40B in new commitments, backlog jumps to $99.4B, but net loss widens to $740M; capex guide raised to $31-$35B
summary: CRWV signed >$40B of new contracts in Q1, lifting contracted revenue backlog from $66.8B to $99.4B. Capex hit $6.8B in the quarter alone and full-year capex was nudged up to $31-$35B. Net loss widened to $740M from $452M in Q4 and $315M a year earlier. Shares fell ~11% Friday on the print as financing-cost and burn-rate concerns offset the backlog growth. Mirrors the CRWV financing-watch flag from yesterday's brief.

headline: Vistra Q1 crushes: EPS $2.87 vs $1.32, EBITDA $1.49B, dividend hiked, FY26 EBITDA guide $6.8-$7.6B reaffirmed
summary: VST reported Q1 net income of $1.03B and ongoing-ops adj EBITDA of $1.494B (vs $5.22B revenue consensus, actual $5.64B). The board hiked the quarterly dividend to $0.229/share. ~$6.3B of buybacks since Nov 2021 (~30% of shares retired); ~$1.5B authorization remaining through year-end 2027. Reaffirmed FY26 EBITDA $6.8-$7.6B and FCFbG $3.925-$4.725B. Confirms AI-power thesis is converting to cash.

headline: Siemens Energy raises FY26 outlook: revenue growth to 14-16%, net income ~EUR 4B, order backlog at record EUR 146B
summary: ENR.DE pre-released Q2 FY26 results and raised full-year guidance: comparable revenue growth 14-16% (vs 11-13%), margin 10-12% (vs 9-11%), net income ~EUR 4B (vs EUR 3-4B), free cash flow pre-tax ~EUR 8B (vs EUR 4-5B). Q2 orders EUR 17.75B (+29.5%), driven by AI-driven grid and gas turbine demand. Backlog at EUR 146B covers ~90% of FY26 and >70% of FY27 revenue. Full Q2 report due May 12.

headline: White House designates power grid a national defense priority; ETN, GEV, NEE, VRT seen as primary beneficiaries
summary: A Trump executive order elevates power generation and grid networks to a national-security category, opening the door to expedited permitting and likely public co-investment in transmission. Eaton (ETN), GE Vernova (GEV), Vertiv (VRT) and NextEra (NEE) flagged as direct beneficiaries via grid manufacturing, gas/wind/nuclear gear, and renewables capacity. Reinforces the multi-year US grid capex narrative.

headline: Rambus -23% on Baird downgrade; HBM diversion squeezes RDIMM supply, signaling severe DRAM tightness through 2027
summary: Baird cut RMBS to Neutral with a $120 PT, flagging that the bulk of incoming DRAM capacity is being routed to HBM, leaving conventional RDIMM/MRDIMM increasingly supply-constrained. Implication for the dashboard: bullish for memory makers with HBM exposure (000660.KS, 005930.KS, MU) and supports the AI-memory super-cycle thesis, but bearish for unit-leveraged business models. Baird models RDIMM unit growth slowing from 20% in 2026 to 12-15% in 2027.

headline: Nebius (NBIS) Q1 due Wednesday; consensus expects 600%+ YoY revenue growth to ~$388M, focus on ARR run-rate and Microsoft/Meta deployments
summary: Neocloud peer Nebius reports Q1 pre-market Wednesday May 13. Street expects ~$388.6M revenue (+600% YoY) and a $0.77 loss per share. Key watch items: trajectory toward $7-9B FY26 ARR target, capex run-rate ($16-20B), and progress on the Meta/Microsoft hyperscaler builds plus the $643M Eigen AI integration. Read-through for CRWV and the broader neocloud trade given Friday's CRWV sell-off.
